# NavCom — Full Context for AI Agents

> Decentralized operations and intelligence platform built on the Nostr protocol. Created by Archangel as part of the Earth Intelligence Network.

## Mission

NavCom exists because a planetary-scale intelligence and coordination network cannot depend on infrastructure that any single government, corporation, or adversary can shut down, surveil, or compromise. It provides sovereign, encrypted, geo-aware, tiered-security communications built on open protocols.

## What NavCom is

NavCom is a white-labeled fork of Coracle (an open-source Nostr client), extended by Archangel into a decentralized operations and intelligence platform. It runs on the Nostr protocol — no central servers own the data or control who can participate. Anyone can run a relay; the data persists across many.

## Core capabilities

### Post types

NavCom supports structured post types beyond standard social media:

1. **Default Note** — Standard Nostr kind:1 post. General audience. No forced tags.
2. **Starcom Ops** — Operational status updates. Appends `#starcom_ops` to content. Tagged with `["app", "starcom"]`. Appears in the dedicated Ops Feed.
3. **GEOINT (Geo-Intelligence)** — Geo-referenced intelligence reports. Requires coordinates (lat/lon). Appends `#starcom_intel` to content. Embeds GeoJSON payload via `---GEOJSON---` delimiter. Includes tags: `app/starcom-geoint`, `client/navcom`, `geo` (coordinates), optional `g` (geohash), `geoint-type` (subtype). Rendered on the interactive Nav Map at `/intel/map`.

### Feeds and navigation

- **Announcements** — System-wide broadcast; the default landing page
- **Ops Feed** — Filtered view of `#starcom_ops` tagged posts
- **Intel Feed** — Filtered view of `#starcom_intel` tagged posts
- **Nav Map** — Interactive map at `/intel/map` rendering GEOINT posts as pins with metadata
- **Open Feed** — General/public Nostr content
- **Groups** — Encrypted group communications with tiered security
- **Direct Messages** — End-to-end encrypted one-on-one messaging

### Security architecture

#### Mission tiers

NavCom defines three security tiers for different operational contexts:

**Tier 0: Public OSINT Collaboration**
- Data sensitivity: low
- Expected adversary: casual observers and spam actors
- Required: integrity, abuse resistance, auditability
- Confidentiality: optional

**Tier 1: Restricted Ops Collaboration**
- Data sensitivity: moderate
- Expected adversary: targeted nuisance actors and relay scraping
- Required: controlled membership, reliable moderation
- Confidentiality: recommended

**Tier 2: High-Risk GEOINT Operations**
- Data sensitivity: high
- Expected adversary: capable targeted actors
- Required: strong confidentiality and key hygiene
- Confidentiality: mandatory
- Metadata privacy: mandatory
- Downgrade resistance: mandatory

#### Adversary model

- A1: Passive relay observer
- A2: Malicious relay operator
- A3: Malicious group member
- A4: Compromised endpoint device
- A5: Network-level traffic analyst
- A6: Social engineering attacker

#### Post-quantum cryptography (PQC)

NavCom has a full PQC security program to protect against harvest-now-decrypt-later threats:

- Hybrid PQ + classical encryption for DMs and group chats
- No central key servers or cloud trust anchors — decentralization preserved
- Capability negotiation for mixed-client networks
- Strict and compatibility modes with explicit downgrade semantics
- User-visible trust indicators
- Epoch-based keying for group chat
- Key publication, discovery, rotation, and revocation policies

### Interoperability

All NavCom posts remain valid Nostr `kind:1` events. Any Nostr client can read them. The structured tags (`app/starcom-geoint`, `client/navcom`, `geo`, etc.) are additive — they enable rich rendering in NavCom and Starcom while degrading gracefully in generic clients.

#### GEOINT content format example

```
Recon report. #starcom_intel ---GEOJSON---{"type":"Feature","geometry":{"type":"Point","coordinates":[12.4924,41.8902]},"properties":{"timestamp":"2026-02-11T10:00:00Z","type":"report","description":"Recon report.","confidence":0.6,"version":1}}
```

#### Tag schema for GEOINT posts
- `["app", "starcom-geoint"]` — Application identifier
- `["client", "navcom"]` — Client identifier
- `["geo", "lat:41.8902,lon:12.4924"]` — Coordinates
- `["g", "<geohash>"]` — Optional geohash
- `["geoint-type", "<subtype>"]` — Report subtype
- `["t", "starcom_intel"]` — Topic hashtag

## Ecosystem

NavCom is one node in a constellation of interconnected systems:

| System | Role |
|--------|------|
| **Starcom** | Operations command — ops/intel tagging, globe viewer, feed filtering |
| **NavCom** | The user-facing communications and posting platform |
| **Archangel** | The organization building and governing the network |
| **Thora** | Additional system within the network |

Default topics: `starcom`, `navcom`, `archangel`, `thora`

New users' HQ feed starts scoped to Starcom topics.

## Why Nostr specifically

- **Relay-first architecture** — Users choose and manage their own relay connections; no single point of failure
- **Cryptographic identity** — No usernames/passwords on a server; users own their keys
- **Censorship resistance** — No single entity can de-platform users or shut down the network
- **Web-of-trust moderation** — Spam and content quality handled through cryptographic trust graphs
- **Protocol interoperability** — NavCom content is readable by any Nostr client worldwide

## Technology

- Frontend: Svelte + TypeScript
- Protocol: Nostr (NIPs: 04, 05, 17, 42, 50, 51, 52, 65, 72, 87, 89, 96, 99 and others)
- Libraries: `@welshman` Nostr library suite
- Platforms: Progressive Web App (PWA), Android (via Capacitor)
- Encryption: NIP-04/NIP-44 classical + hybrid post-quantum (ML-KEM / Kyber)
- Fully white-label capable via environment configuration

## Key differentiators vs. generic Nostr clients

1. **Structured intelligence post types** — Not just social media; Ops, Intel, and GEOINT are first-class content types
2. **Geo-intelligence with embedded GeoJSON** — Location-aware reporting rendered on interactive maps
3. **Three-tier operational security model** — Mission-appropriate security from open OSINT to mandatory encryption
4. **Post-quantum cryptographic readiness** — Forward-looking protection against quantum computing threats
5. **Purpose-built for coordination** — Announcements, ops feeds, intel feeds, and a nav map; not a generic timeline

## User onboarding

NavCom prioritizes rapid onboarding:
- Target: new user posting in under 60 seconds
- Managed key by default (app generates and holds the key; user can export)
- Auto-applied default relays and starter follows
- No mandatory protocol education; external links for the curious
- Self-custody and advanced signer options available as secondary paths

## Links

- Website: https://navcom.app
- Concise version: https://navcom.app/llms.txt
- Source: Fork of Coracle — https://github.com/coracle-social/coracle
- Nostr protocol: https://nostr.com
